summary: Merged a header-focused cleanup PR that pulled most header file renames and some simpler refactors from PR #766. The available slice contains no changed-file list, diff, or review comments, so the exact symbols, file paths, and refactors cannot be verified from this record.

smart_moves: Separated header renames and simpler refactors from the larger PR #766, making the changes easier to review and merge independently.
Reduced scope by extracting low-risk structural cleanup rather than bundling it with larger decompilation or behavioral changes.
decomp_lessons: Large rename/refactor branches can be split into smaller PRs to land non-behavioral cleanup earlier.
Header file renames are useful as standalone maintenance when they improve project organization without requiring matching-sensitive code changes.
When reusing work from a larger PR, document the source PR number so future reviewers can trace provenance.
